topic: A student discovers their teacher follows them on social media
raw_output:
[story 1]

Liam tapped his foot against the worn linoleum floor, the rhythmic drumming a counterpoint to the silence in the empty classroom. He’d been waiting for Professor Albright to finish grading the essays for nearly an hour. Every tick of the clock seemed to stretch into an eternity. He pulled out his phone, intending to check a message, but his thumb hovered over the familiar icon of the professor’s profile. A flicker of something—annoyance mixed with a strange curiosity—made him tap it anyway. There, a recent post about a weekend hike. Liam scrolled down, his jaw tightening. Then he saw it: a notification indicating Professor Albright had just liked one of his previous, slightly clumsy submissions. Liam blinked, a sharp intake of breath escaping him. He quickly minimized the app, his fingers shaking slightly as he tried to steady his breathing. He just needed to get back to his own work.

[story 2]

Maya shifted in her chair, the cheap plastic groaning under her weight. The lecture had dragged on, the drone of the professor’s voice grating on her nerves. She glanced at her phone, the screen illuminating her face in the dim hallway light. She was expecting a reminder, perhaps an email about an assignment extension. Instead, a notification popped up—a direct message from Professor Albright. Maya frowned, her brow furrowing deeply. She clicked it, expecting a professional query, but the message was a casual photo from the previous evening, taken near the campus library. A slow burn started in her chest. She reread the caption, the casual tone grating against her mounting frustration. She quickly closed the message, shoving the phone into her bag with unnecessary force, needing the distraction of a textbook to anchor her attention.

[story 3]

From the moment the bell rang, Sarah was vibrating with restless energy. She paced the length of the corridor, her heels clicking a rapid, uneven rhythm against the tile. She was supposed to be reviewing notes for tomorrow’s presentation, but her focus kept fracturing. She pulled out her device, needing a quick mental break, and navigated to the faculty page. Her eyes scanned the feed, searching for anything that would break the monotony. When she spotted Professor Davies’ profile, she paused. There, a picture of him laughing, clearly taken at a social gathering, was tagged in a story update. Sarah stopped pacing abruptly. Her breath hitched. A strange, hot flush crept up her neck. She stared at the screen, her hands gripping her phone so tightly her knuckles were white. She didn't move to open the message, just stood there, a knot of sudden, sharp feeling tightening in her stomach.
